testing_note: Production URL (stride-five-sigma.vercel.app) is running a pre-iteration-106 build — sidebar shows only 6 nav items (Workflows, List View, Teams, People, Tools, Settings), /prioritization renders as canvas view, and Delete Workspace shows native confirm(). All 7 Playwright actions were used to confirm this. Remaining verification done via static code review of the changed source files on the current branch.

criteria_results.criterion: Step detail panel shows effort (1-5) and impact (1-5) score selectors below maturity section, reusing MATURITY_OPTIONS button pattern (click to set, click again to clear)
criteria_results.evidence: SCORE_OPTIONS array (1-5, Very Low–Very High) confirmed at step-detail-panel.tsx:57-63. Effort section at lines 435-459 using SCORE_OPTIONS with handleFieldUpdate('effort_score', ...) toggle. Impact section at lines 461-485 using same. Both placed below maturity section (after Separator at line 433). Effort uses orange #F97316, Impact uses var(--brand) — matching spec.

criteria_results.criterion: Touchpoint detail panel shows same effort/impact score selectors (reuse same pattern)
criteria_results.evidence: BUILD_RESULT_1.json confirms touchpoint-detail-panel.tsx +54 lines, 0 removed. Builder notes: 'same selectors after gain_score using pain/gain button style.' TypeScript compilation passes (0 errors).

criteria_results.criterion: Score changes persist via existing PATCH routes (effort_score, impact_score fields already in EDITABLE_FIELDS)
criteria_results.evidence: step-detail-panel.tsx line 445: handleFieldUpdate('effort_score', ...) and line 471: handleFieldUpdate('impact_score', ...). EDITABLE_FIELDS plumbing pre-existed from iter 105; no new API routes needed. TypeScript passes.

criteria_results.criterion: New page at /w/[workspaceId]/prioritization with server component (page.tsx) fetching steps+touchpoints+sections+stages+tabs
criteria_results.evidence: prioritization/page.tsx created (+69 lines per BUILD_RESULT_1.json). Builder notes: 'server component fetches steps+touchpoints+sections+stages+tabs'. Build output confirms dynamic server-rendered route. TypeScript passes.

criteria_results.criterion: Client view component (prioritization-view.tsx) renders quadrant chart using CSS absolute positioning — NO charting library
criteria_results.evidence: prioritization-view.tsx: no charting library imported (only @radix-ui/react-tooltip, react, next/navigation). Chart uses CSS absolute positioning within relative container (style={{ height: '480px' }}) at line 177. Quadrant tint divs use absolute positioning at lines 179-182.

criteria_results.criterion: X-axis = effort (1-5), Y-axis = impact (1-5). Quadrant labels: Quick Wins, Major Projects, Fill-Ins, Deprioritize — correct mapping
criteria_results.evidence: Labels confirmed at prioritization-view.tsx lines 189-200. Dot positioning: xPct=((effort-1)/4)*100 (effort 1→left, 5→right), yPct=((5-impact)/4)*100 (impact 5→top, 1→bottom). Quick Wins top-left, Major Projects top-right, Fill-Ins bottom-left, Deprioritize bottom-right — all correct.

criteria_results.criterion: Items plotted as dots colored by parent section/stage using deterministic name hash → HSL palette (8 colors). Radix Tooltip on hover shows item name
criteria_results.evidence: hashColor() at lines 19-23: sums char codes, mod 8, picks from 8-color HSL_PALETTE. Tooltip via TooltipPrimitive.Provider+Root+Trigger+Content at lines 203-243. Tooltip shows item name, group_name, effort/impact scores.

criteria_results.criterion: Click dot navigates to correct canvas tab
criteria_results.evidence: prioritization-view.tsx line 219: onClick={() => router.push('/w/${workspaceId}/${item.tab_id}')}. Each PlotItem carries tab_id from its parent step/touchpoint.

criteria_results.criterion: Two filter dropdowns above chart: Tab (select, default All) and Section/Stage (select, filtered by selected tab, default All)
criteria_results.evidence: Tab select at lines 129-140, Section/Stage select at lines 142-155. groupOptions useMemo at lines 61-78 filters sections by tab_id (process canvas) or stages (journey canvas). useEffect at lines 57-59 resets selectedGroupId when selectedTabId changes.

criteria_results.criterion: Sidebar nav link added for Prioritization (between Perspectives and Runbooks)
criteria_results.evidence: sidebar.tsx line 45: { label: 'Prioritization', icon: Target, href: '/prioritization' }. Position: after Perspectives (line 44), before Comments (line 46) and Runbooks (line 47). Target icon imported at line 22. Active-state exclusion for /prioritization added to Workflows check (line 39).

criteria_results.criterion: workspace-shell.tsx reserved paths includes 'prioritization'
criteria_results.evidence: workspace-shell.tsx line 47: array includes 'prioritization' in the exclusion list. URL /w/{id}/prioritization will NOT be treated as a tabId — isCanvasView will be false, TabBar hidden. Production confirmed the OLD code WITHOUT 'prioritization' in exclusion array (canvas rendered at that URL).

criteria_results.criterion: Only ONE step count display per runbook card (keep progress bar label, remove metadata row count)
criteria_results.evidence: runbooks-list-view.tsx metadata row (lines 111-114): shows section name and date only — no step count. Progress bar section (lines 116-123): shows '{resolved} / {total}' text at line 122 (span.tabular-nums). BUILD_RESULT_2.json confirms 2 lines removed (the span and preceding separator dot).

criteria_results.criterion: Progress bar still shows '{resolved} / {total}' text
criteria_results.evidence: runbooks-list-view.tsx line 122: {resolved} / {total} present alongside progress bar. Untouched.

criteria_results.criterion: Metadata row no longer shows '{resolved}/{total} steps' text
criteria_results.evidence: Metadata row lines 111-114 only contains: section name truncated, separator dot, and formatted date. No step count span present.

criteria_results.criterion: Visual balance maintained — no awkward gaps from removal
criteria_results.evidence: Metadata row: section name · date. Two-element layout is clean. Progress bar row remains below providing count context. No empty separators or orphaned dots.

criteria_results.criterion: Delete Workspace uses Radix Dialog instead of native confirm()
criteria_results.evidence: settings/page.tsx lines 292-310: Dialog open={confirmDeleteOpen} with full Radix Dialog structure. handleDelete() (line 157-159) now calls setConfirmDeleteOpen(true) only. handleConfirmDelete() (lines 161-174) contains the actual delete logic. Production confirmed OLD code: native confirm() still active there, proving the new branch has the replacement.

criteria_results.criterion: Dialog includes workspace name: 'Permanently delete "{workspace.name}"? This cannot be undone.'
criteria_results.evidence: settings/page.tsx line 298: DialogDescription contains 'Permanently delete "{workspace.name}"? This cannot be undone.' — matches spec exactly.

criteria_results.criterion: Cancel button (secondary variant) and Delete button (destructive variant/red)
criteria_results.evidence: settings/page.tsx line 303: Button variant='secondary' for Cancel. Line 305: Button variant='destructive' loading={deleting} for Delete. Mirrors clone dialog pattern exactly.

criteria_results.criterion: Follows same pattern as clone dialog (IMP-028) — confirmDeleteOpen useState boolean
criteria_results.evidence: settings/page.tsx line 42: const [confirmDeleteOpen, setConfirmDeleteOpen] = React.useState(false). Dialog at lines 292-310 mirrors clone dialog structure at lines 273-290.

criteria_results.criterion: Zero window.confirm() calls remain in settings page
criteria_results.evidence: settings/page.tsx line 379 (inside PerspectivesSection.handleDelete): 'if (!confirm("Delete this perspective? All annotations will be permanently removed.")) return;' — a native confirm() call remains. This is pre-existing code, not introduced by IMP-051, but technically violates the 'zero confirm() calls' criterion.

quality_gate.notes: 1 console error: favicon.ico 404 (pre-existing, non-blocking). Pages load correctly on production. Mega listener not injected due to production predating iteration 106 changes (testing pivoted to static review).

bugs_found.description: Native confirm() still used for perspective deletion (PerspectivesSection.handleDelete at settings/page.tsx line 379). Criterion IMP-051 specifies 'zero window.confirm() calls remain in settings page' but one remains. Pre-existing issue, not introduced by IMP-051.
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Navigate to workspace settings. 2. Add a perspective. 3. Click the delete (trash) icon on the perspective row. 4. Observe: native browser confirm() dialog appears instead of Radix Dialog.

improvements_found.observation: Prioritization chart has no axis tick marks or grid lines — users must mentally interpolate where scores 1-4 land on the continuous scale. Only score 1 (leftmost) and score 5 (rightmost) are implicitly anchored at edges.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H6: Recognition rather than recall — visible reference points reduce cognitive load
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Add subtle vertical/horizontal grid lines at 25%, 50%, 75% positions or numeric labels (1–5) along chart edges.

improvements_found.observation: When no items have scores, the empty state message is helpful but there's no direct link or CTA to navigate to a step/canvas to assign scores — user must manually navigate.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H3: User control and freedom — reduce friction for completing prerequisite actions
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Add a secondary action link 'Go to Canvas' pointing to the first workflow tab so users can quickly open a step panel.

improvements_found.observation: Perspective deletion uses native confirm() while workspace delete now uses a polished Radix Dialog — inconsistency within the same settings page creates jarring UX variation.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H4: Consistency and standards — dialogs should use the same pattern throughout the product
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Replace the perspective confirm() at settings/page.tsx line 379 with a Radix Dialog following the same IMP-051 pattern. Would also resolve the P2 bug above.
